commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs maintainer review because the PR is protected, lacks after-fix real Computer Use proof, and changes an approval boundary.; Needs attention: The patch broadens a command/tool approval boundary and should tighten the Computer Use server identification before merge. (sha=026f2c2e5c78f52aaf68c2b7bfc571a5c0a58df3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR routes Codex app-server Computer Use MCP elicitations through plugin approvals, enables MCP elicitation delegation when Computer Use is configured, adds regression coverage, and documents Slack `/approve`.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main's generic bridge requires explicit MCP approval metadata and otherwise falls through to default decline, while the PR tests model the Computer Use no-metadata shape. I did not run a live Computer Use flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer review because the PR is protected, lacks after-fix real Computer Use proof, and changes an approval boundary.

target.title: fix(codex): bridge computer use elicitations
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Computer Use app-approval elicitations from the Codex app-server could fall through to the default declined MCP elicitation response because they do not carry Codex MCP tool-call approval metadata.
- Solution: Add a narrow Computer Use server-name branch that routes those app approvals through the existing plugin approval bridge and preserves existing response/persistence mapping.
- What changed: Computer Use elicitations whose `serverName` contains `computer-use` now enter the bridge; Computer Use startup enables `approvalPolicy.granular.mcp_elicitations`; Slack native command docs include `/approve`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Generic MCP elicitations without approval metadata remain unhandled unless they are Computer Use, and custom Computer Use MCP server names that omit `computer-use` remain out of scope.

## Motivation

- Computer Use needs app approvals such as "Allow Codex to use Notes?" to reach the existing approval UI instead of being returned to the MCP server as a default decline.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Computer Use MCP elicitations for app approval should no longer default-decline before the approval UI can answer.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout in `/Users/kevinlin/code/openclaw`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`; `pnpm build`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Focused Vitest output reported `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 219 passed (219)`; build completed through `write-cli-compat` without errors.
- Observed result after fix: Unit coverage confirms the Computer Use request shape returns `accept`, preserves allow-always persistence, rejects non-Computer Use lookalikes, and sets `approvalPolicy.granular.mcp_elicitations` when Computer Use is enabled.
- What was not tested: Live dev-profile Notes opening was not rerun after this final commit because the operator requested pushing the code immediately.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Prior traces showed `computer-use` elicitations returning `not_plugin`, then falling through to default decline.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Computer Use app approvals are not represented in `pluginAppPolicyContext` and do not include `_meta.codex_approval_kind: "mcp_tool_call"`, so they missed both native plugin policy resolution and the strict generic MCP approval bridge.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No focused test covered Computer Use app-approval elicitations without Codex MCP tool-call metadata.
- Contributing context (if known): Native plugin elicitations and Computer Use app approvals use different ownership/config surfaces.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts`; `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: Computer Use empty-schema app approvals route through plugin approval outcomes and Computer Use startup enables MCP elicitation delegation.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It directly exercises the app-server bridge and thread-start policy boundary without depending on live desktop automation.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None for the Computer Use no-metadata elicitation shape.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Computer Use app approval prompts can be accepted through the existing approval bridge instead of defaulting to decline.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
computer-use elicitation -> plugin resolver: not_plugin -> generic bridge rejects missing metadata -> default decline

After:
computer-use elicitation -> plugin resolver: not_plugin -> Computer Use branch -> plugin approval UI -> accept/decline response
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Computer Use app approvals can now proceed only after the existing approval decision path returns allow. The generic MCP approval bridge remains strict, and the new branch is gated to server names containing `computer-use`.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Local source checkout, Node/pnpm repo wrapper
- Model/provider: N/A for unit/build proof
- Integration/channel (if any): Codex app-server, Computer Use MCP elicitation bridge
- Relevant config (redacted): Computer Use enabled in the run-attempt test fixture

### Steps

1. Run `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`.
2. Run `pnpm build`.

### Expected

- Computer Use app approvals are handled by the approval bridge.
- Focused tests pass.
- Build passes.

### Actual

- Focused tests passed: 2 files, 219 tests.
- Build passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Computer Use allow-once, allow-always persistence, non-Computer Use rejection, non-empty schema decline, schema fallback, fallback title sanitization, and run-attempt MCP elicitation policy enabling.
- Edge cases checked: Missing/non-object schema, empty object schema, sanitized control characters in message/server name.
- What you did **not** verify: Live Computer Use opening Notes after the final commit.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Server-name matching could include unexpected server names containing `computer-use`.
  - Mitigation: This is intentionally limited to the observed Computer Use naming contract and does not relax the generic MCP approval metadata path.
target.author: kevinslin

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
